source: OECD (2024), A Sectoral Taxonomy of AI Intensity, OECD Artificial Intelligence Papers No.30 - NACE A38 'AI intensity summary indicator' (High/Medium/Low); cross-referenced with Felten, Raj & Seamans (2021), AI Industry Exposure (AIIE), Strategic Management Journal 42(12):2195-2217.
method: OECD A38 AI-intensity classes scored High=0.90 / Medium=0.55 / Low=0.25 and averaged across the A38 sub-sectors falling in each of the 6 NACE-aggregated sectors. Manufacturing (Industry) is mostly Low/Medium with only Computer & electronics High -> 0.45 (AI exposure is cognitive, not robotic). Agriculture and public/education/health sit outside the OECD paper's manufacturing+business-services scope; assigned Low / Medium respectively from the Felten et al. AIOE/AIIE consensus.

mapping.Agriculture: NACE A (outside OECD scope; Low per AIOE) = 0.25
mapping.Industry: NACE B-E A38 manuf: 5 Low, 6 Medium, 1 High (Computer&electronics) -> avg 0.45
mapping.Distribution & transport: NACE G-I: Wholesale&retail M, Transport M, Hotels L -> avg 0.45
mapping.ICT, finance & business: NACE J-N: Media/Telecom/IT/Finance/Legal/R&D High, RealEstate/Other/Admin Medium -> avg 0.80
mapping.Public & other services: NACE O-S (outside OECD scope; Medium per AIOE) = 0.55
